body: If we don't have local e2e tests we also should add them. And we should use our library https://github.com/link-foundation/browser-commander, and report for any missing features in it if we find these. The reason I need e2e tests is that for multiple iterations already, we still don't have fully functioning rust-web-box. It still does not allow to do simplest thing - execute `cargo run` or `tree` inside WebVM terminal. That should be fully e2e tests, integration tested and so on. In Safari it is like this: ``` [Log] WARN – "Ignoring the error while validating workspace folder webvm:/workspace - ENOPRO: No file system provider found for resourc…" (workbench.web.main.js, line 657) "Ignoring the error while validating workspace folder webvm:/workspace - ENOPRO: No file system provider found for resource 'webvm:/workspace'" [Warning] The web worker extension host is started in a same-origin iframe! (workbench.web.main.js, line 1886) [Log] INFO – "Found additional builtin location extensions in env" – Array (2) (workbench.web.main.js, line 657) Array (2) [Log] INFO – "Ignoring fetching additional builtin extensions from gallery as it is disabled." (workbench.web.main.js, line 657) [Log] INFO – "Ignoring fetching additional builtin extensions from gallery as it is disabled." (workbench.web.main.js, line 657, x6) [Error] Source Map loading errors (x7) [Error] Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 () (layout.contribution.darwin.js.map, line 0) [Error] Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 () (workbench.js.map, line 0) [Error] Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 () (main.js.map, line 0) [Error] Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 () (xterm.js.map, line 0) [Error] Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 () (addon-unicode11.js.map, line 0) [Error] Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 () (addon-clipboard.js.map, line 0) [Error] Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 () (addon-webgl.js.map, line 0) [Log] INFO – "Ignoring fetching additional builtin extensions from gallery as it is disabled." (workbench.web.main.js, line 657) [Log] INFO – "Ignoring fetching additional builtin extensions from gallery as it is disabled." (workbench.web.main.js, line 657) [Log] INFO – "Ignoring fetching additional builtin extensions from gallery as it is disabled." (workbench.web.main.js, line 657, x2) [Log] INFO – "Updating additional builtin extensions cache" (workbench.web.main.js, line 657) [Error] Cancelled load to https://link-foundation.github.io/rust-web-box/vscode-web/out/vs/workbench/contrib/welcomeGettingStarted/common/media/dark-hc.png because it violates the resource's Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy response header. [Error] Cannot load image https://link-foundation.github.io/rust-web-box/vscode-web/out/vs/workbench/contrib/welcomeGettingStarted/common/media/dark-hc.png due to access control checks. [Error] Failed to load resource: Cancelled load to https://link-foundation.github.io/rust-web-box/vscode-web/out/vs/workbench/contrib/welcomeGettingStarted/common/media/dark-hc.png because it violates the resource's Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy response header. (dark-hc.png, line 0) [Error] Cancelled load to https://link-foundation.github.io/rust-web-box/vscode-web/out/vs/workbench/contrib/welcomeGettingStarted/common/media/dark.png because it violates the resource's Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy response header. [Error] Cannot load image https://link-foundation.github.io/rust-web-box/vscode-web/out/vs/workbench/contrib/welcomeGettingStarted/common/media/dark.png due to access control checks. [Error] Failed to load resource: Cancelled load to https://link-foundation.github.io/rust-web-box/vscode-web/out/vs/workbench/contrib/welcomeGettingStarted/common/media/dark.png because it violates the resource's Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy response header. (dark.png, line 0) [Error] Cancelled load to https://link-foundation.github.io/rust-web-box/vscode-web/out/vs/workbench/contrib/welcomeGettingStarted/common/media/light.png because it violates the resource's Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy response header. (light.png, line 0) [Error] Cancelled load to https://link-foundation.github.io/rust-web-box/vscode-web/out/vs/workbench/contrib/welcomeGettingStarted/common/media/light-hc.png because it violates the resource's Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy response header. [Error] Cannot load image https://link-foundation.github.io/rust-web-box/vscode-web/out/vs/workbench/contrib/welcomeGettingStarted/common/media/light-hc.png due to access control checks. [Error] Failed to load resource: Cancelled load to https://link-foundation.github.io/rust-web-box/vscode-web/out/vs/workbench/contrib/welcomeGettingStarted/common/media/light-hc.png because it violates the resource's Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy response header. (light-hc.png, line 0) [Log] INFO – "Ignoring fetching additional builtin extensions from gallery as it is disabled." (workbench.web.main.js, line 657) [Log] WARN – "No search provider registered for scheme: webvm, waiting" (workbench.web.main.js, line 657) [Error] TypeError: undefined is not an object (evaluating 'c.a4.a1') y8 (cx_esm.js:1:190555) y_ (cx_esm.js:1:187731) [Error] CheerpException: Program exited with code 71 (a1d0a4ae-412d-47d7-92ef-bb18089aad00, line 1) ``` So previously we were at least have bash terminal working, and ls was working, but no commands: https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-web-box/issues/13 And instead of fixing these everything got broken. That is why it is so critical to cover everything we can we tests, so we build on solid foundation, and it is impossible to get things broken again. Use all the best practices from CI/CD templates (check full file tree to compare for all GitHub workflow and CI/CD scripts file), if the same issue is found in template report issue also in templates: - https://github.com/link-foundation/js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template - https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template We should compare all files, so we don't have more CI/CD errors in the future and reuse all the best practices from these templates. We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions). If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration. If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code. Please plan and execute everything in a single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context autocompacts and you can continue indefinetely, do as much as possible in one go, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done. Use your own Playwright MCP locally to debug everything manually in Google Chrome and other browsers as well. Support for Google Chrome is critical, but other browsers should work also if possible. All experience you collect in manual debugging make sure to pass to CI/CD executed e2e tests.
title: We need real e2e tests, that are executed after GitHub Pages publish on https://link-foundation.github.io/rust-web-box directly, not only local e2e tests
